title: Delicate Yeasty Spanish Split Peas Fideua
description: A quick and flavorful Galician dish featuring split peas and a yeasty ferment, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This fideua is a twist on the traditional Spanish recipe, with a velvety texture and a dash of spice.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the split peas, yeast, and water to the skillet, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the peas are tender.
Use a fork to mash some of the peas and create a thick, velvety texture.
Serve the fideua hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
